title: Add PodDisruptionBudgets before an EKS version upgrade
scenario: You're upgrading from EKS 1.28 to 1.29. Before draining nodes, you must ensure stateful workloads can tolerate the drain. The `postgres` Deployment (3 replicas) has a PDB with `maxUnavailable: 2` — too permissive (allows 2 of 3 pods down simultaneously). Fix the postgres PDB and add one for the `redis` Deployment.
starterYaml:
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: postgres-pdb
  namespace: default
spec:
  maxUnavailable: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: postgres

hints.text: `maxUnavailable: 2` on a 3-replica postgres means 2 pods can go down simultaneously — that's data loss risk. Use `minAvailable: 2` to guarantee at least 2 replicas stay running.
Remove `maxUnavailable: 2` and replace with `minAvailable: 2`. Then add a second YAML document with the redis PDB.

Redis PDB template:
```yaml
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: redis-pdb
  namespace: default
spec:
  minAvailable: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: redis
```

solution:
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: postgres-pdb
  namespace: default
spec:
  minAvailable: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: postgres
---
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: redis-pdb
  namespace: default
spec:
  minAvailable: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: redis
